title: Replace fixed compound life with a circuit-prior online wear posterior
summary: A 2024 F1 strategy thesis estimates tyre-wear coefficients online using only data up to the current lap and shrinks them with priors from the same circuit in a prior year; a 2026 state-space paper argues latent degradation with pit-stop resets is a better foundation than deterministic wear constants.
rationale: A 2024 F1 strategy thesis estimates tyre-wear coefficients online using only data up to the current lap and shrinks them with priors from the same circuit in a prior year; a 2026 state-space paper argues latent degradation with pit-stop resets is a better foundation than deterministic wear constants. Your current best code still hard-codes compound life windows.
implementation_hint: For each `Race`/`Year`/`Compound` or circuit cluster, fit a robust rolling wear model on non-pit, non-outlier laps only; shrink early-lap estimates toward prior-year circuit coefficients; add posterior wear slope, finish-margin, and uncertainty features to the existing tabular model.
expected_effect: Should reduce circuit/year mismatch and make pit-window features less brittle than static expected-life maps.
risk: Sparse early-race or wet-tyre data can make posteriors noisy; exclude pit-entry/exit and future laps to avoid leakage.
